title: Aurora over the Bitterroot Mountains
explanation: A strong geomagnetic storm brought aurora borealis as far south as Montana in early June, with curtains of green and red light visible above mountain silhouettes in the Bitterroot Valley. Solar maximum conditions continue through 2026.

title: The Milky Way over Glacier National Park
explanation: One of the darkest sky areas in the lower 48 states, Glacier National Park in Montana offers spectacular views of the Milky Way core rising above St. Mary Lake. This panoramic image shows the galactic band stretching from the southern horizon toward the north.

title: Perseverance Selfie in Jezero Crater
explanation: NASA's Perseverance rover took this selfie in Jezero Crater on Mars, showing the rover's MMRTG power system and the dusty delta region where ancient river sediments have preserved conditions potentially hospitable to ancient microbial life.

title: NGC 6334 - The Cat's Paw Nebula
explanation: Glowing clouds of ionized gas and dust in the constellation Scorpius form the Cat's Paw Nebula, a region of active star formation some 5,500 light-years away. New stars are being born within dense molecular cores throughout this region.

title: Rocky Mountains from the International Space Station
explanation: International Space Station crew photographed the northern Rocky Mountains from orbit, capturing the snowpack patterns of the Bitterroot, Cabinet, and Beartooth ranges of Montana. Clark Fork and the Missoula valley are visible from this perspective.

title: Earth's Twin Vortex - DSCOVR View
explanation: The DSCOVR satellite's EPIC camera captured twin storm systems swirling in opposite directions over the Pacific Ocean, a vivid illustration of the Coriolis effect at work on Earth's weather systems.

title: Ingenuity's 80th Flight on Mars
explanation: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter completed its 80th flight on Mars, a testament to the durability of the small rotorcraft well beyond its original 5-flight technology walkthroughnstration mission. Ingenuity now scouts terrain ahead of the Perseverance rover.

title: The Veil Nebula in Cygnus
explanation: The Veil Nebula is the remnant of a massive star that exploded approximately 10,000 years ago. Intricate filaments of shocked gas - seen here in hydrogen and oxygen emission - spread across 110 light-years of space in the constellation Cygnus.

title: Saturn at Opposition 2026
explanation: Saturn reaches opposition in June 2026, appearing at its largest and brightest in night skies. Backyard telescopes will reveal Cassini's Division in the ring system and the planet's polar hexagonal cloud pattern. Saturn rises at sunset this week.

title: Curiosity at the Murray Formation
explanation: Curiosity rover examines sedimentary layers in Gale Crater's Murray Formation, where ancient lake sediments preserve chemical signatures of a once-habitable environment on early Mars. The mission continues to return data after more than 4,900 Martian days on the surface.

title: M13 - The Great Globular Cluster in Hercules
explanation: The Hercules Cluster, M13, contains several hundred thousand stars in a sphere about 145 light-years across, located 25,000 light-years away. On a clear summer night M13 is faintly visible to the naked eye from Montana's dark skies.

title: Noctilucent Clouds over Canada
explanation: Noctilucent clouds - icy clouds at the edge of space around 80 km altitude - were photographed glowing electric blue above the northern horizon in late May. These mesospheric clouds are visible at high latitudes during summer.

title: Artemis III Landing Site Candidates at the Lunar South Pole
explanation: NASA's Artemis III mission site candidates near the lunar south pole include permanently shadowed craters harboring water ice deposits. LRO imagery shows the terrain where human boots may soon leave marks for the first time since Apollo 17 in 1972.

title: The Lagoon Nebula in Infrared - James Webb
explanation: The Lagoon Nebula (M8) in Sagittarius is one of the few nebulae visible to the naked eye. James Webb Space Telescope infrared imagery reveals intricate pillar-like structures where the intense radiation of newborn massive stars sculpts surrounding gas and dust.

title: DSCOVR Earth from a Million Miles
explanation: NASA's DSCOVR satellite captured Earth from the L1 Lagrange point - one million miles from Earth - showing North America on a mostly clear late May day. The Rocky Mountain snowpack is visible as a white spine from New Mexico to Canada.

title: Curiosity Captures a Solar Eclipse on Mars
explanation: Curiosity's Mastcam recorded Mars' moon Phobos transiting the face of the Sun - a Martian solar eclipse. Phobos' irregular potato-shaped outline is visible in this sequence of frames. At this rate Phobos is spiraling inward and will break apart within 50 million years.

title: Rho Ophiuchi Cloud Complex - JWST
explanation: The James Webb Space Telescope captured the Rho Ophiuchi molecular cloud complex in unprecedented detail - young stars blasting jets of material into surrounding gas just 390 light-years away in the constellation Ophiuchus. Nearest star-forming region to Earth.

title: Comet C/2026 A3 Brightens
explanation: Comet C/2026 A3 is brightening as it approaches perihelion in August 2026, with some predictions suggesting naked-eye visibility by late summer. Astrophotographers are capturing spectacular tails of gas and dust as the comet heats up.

title: Beehive Cluster and Planetary Conjunction
explanation: A chance alignment placed Jupiter and Mars in apparent proximity to the Beehive Cluster (M44) in Cancer in late May 2026. The Beehive is one of the nearest open star clusters, approximately 610 light-years away, and contains about 1,000 stars.

title: The Veil Nebula in Hydrogen and Oxygen
explanation: A delicate web of glowing gas marks the expanding remnant of a supernova in Cygnus. This false-color image combines hydrogen-alpha emission (red) and doubly-ionized oxygen emission (blue) to reveal intricate shock structures in the 10,000-year-old blast wave.
